intent: Testhaus project-initiation seed from customer PDFs: 1956 German two-family semi-detached house with 19.80 m by 9.90 m footprint, basement, mirrored ground and attic dwellings, central party wall, two stair cores, continuous steep gable roof, paired dormers, rendered masonry walls, and punched facade openings.

toposolids.name: Continuous sloped site host from Ansichten.pdf

materialIntent.evidence: Ansichten.pdf shows light rendered long and gable elevations.
Ansichten.pdf shows a continuous tiled pitched roof.
Long elevations show darker dormer side faces and a dark horizontal band below eaves.
Grundrisse, Schnitt.pdf shows basement and structural floor plates.
Plan partitions are simplified as light interior walls.
Ansichten.pdf shows non-flat ground lines at the long and gable elevations.

viewpoints.name: Long elevation with paired entries and dormers

commands.name: Ground left rear room band wall
Ground left front support band wall
Ground right rear room band wall
Ground right front support band wall
Attic left rear room band wall
Attic right rear room band wall

assumptions.value: Scaled from visible 1:100 plan dimensions, normalized to 19800 mm by 9900 mm.
Openings use createWallOpening because cmd-v3 seed bundle has no createDoor/createWindow command.
The paper elevations show a non-flat site; modeled as one continuous sloped Toposolid host excavated by the basement slab/foundation footprint.
assumptions.source: Ansichten.pdf long elevation and gable elevation ground lines
